features.name: getTemplatePath
features.description: Computes the path to the HTML template file relative to the module location
features.reason: Pure path computation with no side effects; unlikely to break unless file structure changes

features.name: startDashboard server lifecycle
features.description: Creates an HTTP server, listens on specified port, and returns url and stop function
features.reason: Core functionality that starts the server, binds to a port, and must return correct URL; involves OS-level networking

features.name: HTML template serving (/ and /index.html)
features.description: Serves the HTML template file with correct content-type header at root and /index.html paths
features.reason: Involves file system read and HTTP response; standard serving behavior but essential for dashboard usability

features.name: Template load failure error handling
features.description: Returns 500 status when template file cannot be read from disk
features.reason: Error boundary that prevents server crash on missing/unreadable template; users see graceful error instead of hang

features.name: /api/runs endpoint
features.description: Calls listRuns(cwd) and returns JSON-serialized run data with correct content-type
features.reason: Primary data API endpoint; integrates with store module and serializes data over HTTP; critical for dashboard functionality

features.name: /events SSE endpoint
features.description: Establishes Server-Sent Events connection with correct headers, sends connected event, and manages client set
features.reason: Real-time communication channel; must set correct SSE headers, maintain connection, and track clients for broadcast

features.name: SSE client cleanup on disconnect
features.description: Removes client from sseClients set when the request connection closes
features.reason: Prevents memory leaks from accumulating stale client references; requires simulating connection lifecycle

features.name: File watcher triggers SSE broadcast
features.description: Watches runsDir for changes and broadcasts 'runs-updated' event to all connected SSE clients
features.reason: Core real-time update mechanism; combines fs.watch with SSE broadcast to multiple clients; race conditions possible

features.name: File watcher graceful failure
features.description: Silently handles the case when runsDir does not exist (watch throws) without crashing the server
features.reason: Defensive coding for missing directory; server should still start even if runs directory doesn't exist yet

features.name: 404 handling for unknown routes
features.description: Returns 404 status with 'Not found' body for any unrecognized URL path
features.reason: Standard fallback behavior; simple but ensures no unintended route exposure

features.name: stop() cleanup function
features.description: Closes file watcher, ends all SSE connections, clears client set, and closes the HTTP server
features.reason: Resource cleanup preventing port leaks, zombie watchers, and dangling connections; important for test isolation and graceful shutdown

features.name: Server error rejection
features.description: Rejects the returned promise and closes file watcher if server emits an error (e.g., port already in use)
features.reason: Critical error path; callers must receive the error to handle port conflicts or permission issues properly

features.name: Default port and custom port
features.description: Uses port 3847 by default but accepts a custom port parameter; returns the actual bound port in the URL
features.reason: Port configuration affects deployment; actual port from server.address() may differ from requested (e.g., port 0 for random)

features.name: executeWorkflow - dynamic function creation
features.description: Creates an AsyncFunction from the workflow body string with specific parameter names (agent, pipeline, parallel, phase, log, args)
features.reason: Core execution mechanism - dynamically compiling user-provided code into an async function. Incorrect parameter binding would break all workflows.

features.name: executeWorkflow - runtime binding
features.description: Passes runtime properties (agent, pipeline, parallel, phase, log) and args as arguments to the dynamically created function
features.reason: Core business logic ensuring the runtime API is correctly exposed to workflow scripts. Wrong bindings would silently break workflow execution.

features.name: executeWorkflow - async execution
features.description: Executes the dynamically created function and returns its result as a Promise
features.reason: Tests that actual async workflow bodies execute correctly, including await expressions, and that return values propagate properly through the promise chain.

features.name: executeWorkflow - undefined args handling
features.description: Handles the case where args parameter is undefined
features.reason: Edge case where no arguments are passed to a workflow. Should pass undefined without errors.

features.name: executeWorkflow - error propagation
features.description: Errors thrown inside the workflow body should propagate as rejected promises
features.reason: Error handling is critical for debugging workflows. Unhandled or swallowed errors would make workflows impossible to debug.

features.name: executeWorkflow - syntax error in body
features.description: Handles malformed JavaScript in the body string (syntax errors at function creation time)
features.reason: User-provided workflow bodies may contain syntax errors. The system should throw meaningful errors rather than crash unexpectedly.

features.name: AsyncFunction constructor
features.description: The AsyncFunction reference is correctly derived from async function prototype
features.reason: Simple constant derivation that is unlikely to change, but validates the metaprogramming approach works correctly.

features.name: formatRun
features.description: Pure function that formats a WorkflowRun object into a human-readable multi-line string with run ID, workflow name, status, timestamps, steps with durations, and optional result
features.reason: Pure function with no side effects - transforms structured data to string. Handles optional fields (phase, duration, result) and formats timestamps and durations.

features.name: workflow tool - list action
features.description: Lists available workflows from filesystem discovery and recent runs from the store, formatted with source and directory info, plus the script template
features.reason: Depends on listWorkflows (filesystem discovery across multiple dirs) and listRuns (store access). Combines multiple external data sources into formatted output.

features.name: workflow tool - status action
features.description: Retrieves and formats a specific workflow run by run_id, returning error if run_id missing or run not found
features.reason: Requires loading run state from persistent store (loadRun). Needs validation of run_id parameter and handling of missing runs.

features.name: workflow tool - cancel action
features.description: Cancels a workflow run by setting its status to 'cancelled', marking all pending/running steps as cancelled, and persisting the updated state
features.reason: Mutates persistent state (run status and step statuses). Critical for workflow lifecycle management - incorrect cancellation could leave orphaned processes or corrupted state.

features.name: workflow tool - start action (successful execution)
features.description: Loads workflow module, parses args, creates run record, sets up runtime with step tracking, executes workflow, saves completed result
features.reason: Core business logic orchestrating multiple subsystems: module loading, runtime creation, workflow execution, persistent state management, and sub-agent delegation. A failure here breaks the entire plugin.

features.name: workflow tool - start action (workflow not found)
features.description: When requested workflow doesn't exist, returns helpful error with available workflows list, project dir path, and creation template
features.reason: Depends on loadWorkflow and listWorkflows for filesystem discovery, plus mkdir for directory creation. Important for UX but not data-critical.

features.name: workflow tool - start action (error handling)
features.description: Handles workflow execution failures: distinguishes between cancellation (signal abort or specific error message) and general failures, updates run status accordingly
features.reason: Error handling in async execution paths is critical for reliability. Must correctly differentiate cancellation from failure and persist correct state in both cases.

features.name: workflow tool - args JSON parsing
features.description: Parses the optional 'args' string parameter as JSON, returning an error if invalid JSON is provided
features.reason: Input validation logic - parse user-provided JSON string. Invalid input must produce a clear error rather than crashing the workflow start.

features.name: step tracking callback
features.description: Runtime callback that updates existing steps (by name+startedAt match) or adds new ones, then persists the run state asynchronously
features.reason: Core state tracking logic that determines whether a step is new or an update. Incorrect deduplication could cause lost progress or duplicate entries. The find-and-assign logic is subtle.

features.name: session_start - workflow count status
features.description: On session start, discovers workflows in cwd and sets a UI status indicator showing the count
features.reason: Cosmetic UI status update. Depends on filesystem discovery but failure has no functional impact on the extension.

features.name: session_start - ensure project workflow dir
features.description: On session start, creates the project workflow directory (.pi/workflows/) with recursive mkdir, silently catching errors
features.reason: Filesystem side effect with error suppression. Important for first-run experience but failure is gracefully handled and non-critical.

features.name: dashboard command
features.description: Registers a 'dashboard' command that starts a web dashboard (singleton pattern) and notifies the user of the URL, preventing duplicate instances
features.reason: Starts an HTTP server (startDashboard) and manages singleton state. Needs to verify deduplication logic and proper server lifecycle.

features.name: workflow command - interactive selection
features.description: Registers a 'workflow' command that either lists workflows interactively (with ui.select) or sends a user message to trigger the workflow tool with parsed arguments
features.reason: UI interaction layer that delegates to the workflow tool via sendUserMessage. Logic is thin - mostly string parsing and UI calls.

features.name: cancel action - step status filtering
features.description: When cancelling, only steps with 'pending' or 'running' status are set to 'cancelled', leaving completed/failed steps unchanged
features.reason: State transition logic that must preserve completed step results. Incorrectly overwriting completed steps would lose execution history.

features.name: run creation and initialization
features.description: Creates a new WorkflowRun with randomUUID, workflow name, 'running' status, parsed args, timestamps, and empty steps array before execution begins
features.reason: Correct initialization of run state is important for downstream tracking. Verifying the shape and defaults ensures store consistency.

features.name: getProjectWorkflowDir
features.description: Returns the project workflow directory path by joining cwd with '.pi/workflows'
features.reason: Pure function with simple path joining, no side effects or complex logic

features.name: findGitRoot
features.description: Traverses filesystem upward from cwd to locate the nearest .git directory, returns the root or null
features.reason: Performs filesystem stat calls and directory traversal; critical for correct workflow discovery boundaries

features.name: discoverProjectDirs
features.description: Builds list of potential project workflow directories by walking up from cwd to git root, checking standard directory names
features.reason: Depends on findGitRoot and constructs paths relative to filesystem structure; determines which workflows are discoverable

features.name: getAllWorkflowDirs
features.description: Combines discovered project directories with global directories into a single ordered list
features.reason: Orchestrates discoverProjectDirs and appends global dirs; order matters for priority resolution

features.name: listDir - extension filtering
features.description: Reads a directory and filters files to only those with supported extensions (.js, .ts, .mjs, .mts)
features.reason: Reads filesystem via readdir, filters by extension; ensures only valid workflow files are included

features.name: listDir - error handling
features.description: Returns an empty array when the directory does not exist or cannot be read
features.reason: Graceful error handling is critical for robustness when directories don't exist; silent failure prevents crashes

features.name: listWorkflows - deduplication and priority
features.description: Lists all workflows across directories, deduplicating by name with project workflows taking priority over global ones
features.reason: Core discovery feature; project-over-global priority and dedup logic is critical for correct user experience

features.name: listWorkflows - source labeling
features.description: Correctly labels each workflow as 'project' or 'global' based on its directory origin
features.reason: Source labeling affects UI display and user understanding of where workflows come from

features.name: listWorkflows - alphabetical sorting
features.description: Returns workflows sorted alphabetically by name
features.reason: Cosmetic ordering for display purposes; not functionally critical

features.name: extractMeta - valid meta parsing
features.description: Parses 'export const meta = {...}' from source code and returns the meta object with at minimum a name field
features.reason: Core business logic that determines if a workflow file is valid; uses regex + brace matching + Function eval; parsing errors break workflow loading

features.name: extractMeta - nested braces handling
features.description: Correctly handles nested objects within the meta declaration by tracking brace depth
features.reason: Brace-depth algorithm is error-prone; incorrect parsing would silently break workflow discovery for complex meta objects

features.name: extractMeta - missing or invalid meta
features.description: Returns null when source has no meta export, meta doesn't start with '{', meta is unparseable, or meta lacks a name field
features.reason: Error cases must return null gracefully rather than throwing; guards against malformed workflow files

features.name: extractBody
features.description: Removes the 'export const meta = {...}' declaration from source, returning only the workflow body code
features.reason: Pure string transformation using regex; important for correct workflow execution but logic is straightforward

features.name: loadWorkflow - successful loading
features.description: Finds and loads a workflow by name from the first matching directory/extension combination, returning meta and body
features.reason: Primary entry point for workflow execution; reads files from disk and combines extractMeta + extractBody; failure here breaks all workflow usage

features.name: loadWorkflow - not found
features.description: Returns null when no workflow with the given name exists in any searched directory with any valid extension
features.reason: Must gracefully handle missing workflows without throwing; important for error UX

features.name: loadWorkflow - skips files without valid meta
features.description: Continues searching when a file exists but extractMeta returns null (file lacks valid meta export)
features.reason: Ensures partially valid files don't block discovery of valid workflows in later directories

features.name: createEmitResultTool - object schema passthrough
features.description: When schema.type is 'object', the schema is passed directly as tool parameters without wrapping
features.reason: Core logic for structured output extraction. Incorrect schema handling would cause validation failures or data loss.

features.name: createEmitResultTool - non-object schema wrapping
features.description: When schema.type is not 'object' (e.g., array, string), wraps in {type:'object', properties:{result:<schema>}, required:['result']}
features.reason: Ensures Claude's object-only tool parameter constraint is satisfied for all schema types.

features.name: createEmitResultTool - result capture and unwrapping
features.description: execute() captures params directly for object schemas, unwraps .result for non-object schemas, and returns terminate:true
features.reason: Data integrity concern - incorrect unwrapping could corrupt structured output returned to workflows.

features.name: createEmitResultTool - getResult before execute
features.description: getResult() returns undefined before execute() is called
features.reason: Edge case handling for when the model doesn't call the tool.

features.name: createRuntime - log delegates to ctx.ui.notify
features.description: log(message) calls ctx.ui.notify(message, 'info')
features.reason: Simple delegation with no transformation logic.

features.name: createRuntime - phase sets currentPhase
features.description: phase(name) sets the current phase that is used by subsequent agent calls
features.reason: Simple state setter, minimal logic.

features.name: agent - step lifecycle with schema (structured output)
features.description: Creates a step with 'running' status, calls onStep, creates agent session with emit_result tool, processes prompt, captures result, marks step as 'completed'
features.reason: Core workflow execution path involving external agent sessions, tool creation, and state management. Orchestrates multiple components.

features.name: agent - step lifecycle without schema (text response)
features.description: Creates agent session without custom tools, subscribes to message events, captures response text, returns it as result
features.reason: Core workflow execution path involving external agent sessions and event subscription. Primary way workflows interact with LLMs.

features.name: agent - abort signal cancellation before start
features.description: If signal is already aborted when agent() is called, throws 'Workflow cancelled' immediately
features.reason: Important for resource cleanup and preventing unnecessary API calls after cancellation.

features.name: agent - abort signal cancellation during execution
features.description: Registers abort handler that calls session.abort() and session.dispose(), marks step as 'cancelled', throws error
features.reason: Ensures proper cleanup of external agent sessions on cancellation. Memory leak potential if mishandled.

features.name: agent - abort handler cleanup on success
features.description: Removes abort event listener after prompt completes (in finally block) to prevent memory leaks
features.reason: Memory management concern - leaked listeners could accumulate over many workflow steps.

features.name: agent - error handling marks step as failed
features.description: When session.prompt throws (non-abort), step status is set to 'failed' with error message, returns undefined
features.reason: Error resilience - workflows must handle LLM failures gracefully without crashing the entire pipeline.

features.name: agent - emit_result tool not called fallback
features.description: If schema is provided but model doesn't call emit_result, step completes with error message 'emit_result tool was not called' and returns undefined
features.reason: Edge case - defensive handling when model misbehaves. Should rarely happen with terminate:true.

features.name: agent - step name derivation
features.description: Step name uses options.label if provided, otherwise first 60 chars of prompt. Phase uses options.phase or currentPhase.
features.reason: Simple string formatting for display purposes.

features.name: agent - schema prompt construction
features.description: Constructs prompt with schema instructions appended, different wording for object vs non-object schemas
features.reason: Prompt engineering - incorrect instructions could cause model to fail structured output generation.

features.name: pipeline - sequential stage processing
features.description: Processes items through multiple stages sequentially, passing output of each stage as input to the next
features.reason: Core data transformation pipeline. Incorrect chaining would produce wrong results in multi-stage workflows.

features.name: pipeline - individual item failure handling
features.description: When a stage throws for one item, logs the error and returns undefined for that item while continuing others
features.reason: Fault tolerance - one failed item shouldn't crash the entire pipeline. Important for batch processing.

features.name: pipeline - cancellation between stages
features.description: Checks signal.aborted between stages and throws 'Workflow cancelled'
features.reason: Ensures long-running pipelines can be cancelled at stage boundaries.

features.name: pipeline - cancellation within stage tasks
features.description: Checks signal.aborted before and after each task within a stage, throws on cancellation
features.reason: Finer-grained cancellation within parallel stage execution.

features.name: parallel - concurrent execution
features.description: Runs all thunks concurrently via Promise.all and returns results array
features.reason: Core concurrency primitive for workflows. Simple but important for parallelizing agent calls.

features.name: parallel - pre-execution abort check
features.description: If signal is aborted before starting, throws 'Workflow cancelled' without executing thunks
features.reason: Prevents wasted work when workflow is already cancelled.

features.name: createRuntime - returns complete WorkflowRuntime interface
features.description: Returns object with agent, log, phase, parallel, and pipeline functions matching the WorkflowRuntime interface
features.reason: Contract verification - ensures runtime satisfies the type interface expected by workflow scripts.

features.name: saveRun - persist workflow run to filesystem
features.description: Creates the runs directory recursively and writes a WorkflowRun as formatted JSON to a file named by runId
features.reason: Core data persistence function that mutates the filesystem (mkdir + writeFile). Loss of run data would break workflow state tracking entirely.

features.name: loadRun - read workflow run from filesystem
features.description: Reads a JSON file by runId, parses it, and returns the WorkflowRun object. Returns null if the file doesn't exist or is invalid.
features.reason: Filesystem read with JSON parsing and error handling. Critical path for retrieving run state. Error case (returning null) must be verified.

features.name: loadRun - error handling for missing/invalid files
features.description: Returns null when the file does not exist or contains invalid JSON, rather than throwing
features.reason: Silent error handling means callers depend on null return for missing runs. If this throws instead, it could crash workflow status checks.

features.name: listRuns - list and aggregate all workflow runs
features.description: Reads the runs directory, filters for .json files, loads each run, filters out nulls, and sorts by updatedAt descending
features.reason: Orchestrates multiple filesystem operations (readdir + multiple readFile). Aggregation logic with filtering and sorting is critical for correct UI display.

features.name: listRuns - empty/missing directory handling
features.description: Returns an empty array when the runs directory does not exist, rather than throwing
features.reason: Graceful degradation for fresh projects with no runs. If this throws, workflow list commands would crash on first use.

features.name: listRuns - sorting by updatedAt descending
features.description: Results are sorted with most recently updated runs first (b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt)
features.reason: Sort order affects user-facing display. Incorrect ordering would show stale runs first but wouldn't lose data.

features.name: listRuns - filters only .json files
features.description: Only files ending in .json are processed; other files in the directory are ignored
features.reason: Prevents crashes from non-JSON files (e.g., .DS_Store, temp files) in the runs directory.

features.name: runsDir - path construction
features.description: Constructs the correct runs directory path by joining cwd with .pi-workflows/.runs
features.reason: Pure path join function with no branching logic. Unlikely to break unless the constant changes.

features.name: runPath - path construction
features.description: Constructs the correct file path for a run by joining runsDir with {runId}.json
features.reason: Pure path join function. Simple string concatenation with .json extension.

features.name: saveRun - JSON serialization format
features.description: Serializes the run with 2-space indentation and a trailing newline for human readability
features.reason: Formatting is cosmetic. The file is still valid JSON regardless of indentation, but trailing newline follows POSIX conventions.

features.name: saveRun and loadRun roundtrip
features.description: A WorkflowRun saved with saveRun can be loaded back with loadRun and the data is identical
features.reason: End-to-end data integrity test. Serialization/deserialization roundtrip must preserve all fields without data loss or type coercion issues.

features.name: WorkflowStepStatus union type validation
features.description: Validate that WorkflowStepStatus only accepts 'pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed', or 'cancelled' values at runtime boundaries (e.g., deserialization from JSON, API responses)
features.reason: String union types are enforced at compile time but not at runtime boundaries where data enters the system (e.g., from disk, API). Runtime validation guards would need testing.

features.name: WorkflowRun status union type validation
features.description: Validate that WorkflowRun.status only accepts 'running', 'completed', 'failed', or 'cancelled' values at runtime boundaries
features.reason: Status field drives workflow control flow; invalid states at runtime boundaries could cause subtle bugs in workflow execution logic.

features.name: WorkflowStep state transitions
features.description: Verify that WorkflowStep objects transition through valid status sequences (pending→running→completed/failed, not completed→pending)
features.reason: State machine correctness is critical for workflow reliability. Invalid transitions could leave workflows in inconsistent states.

features.name: WorkflowRun temporal consistency
features.description: Verify that createdAt <= updatedAt, and step startedAt <= completedAt invariants hold when constructing or mutating WorkflowRun objects
features.reason: Temporal invariants ensure correct ordering/auditing of workflow execution. Violations indicate logic bugs in workflow management.

features.name: WorkflowMeta structure conformance
features.description: Verify that WorkflowMeta objects parsed from workflow module files have required 'name' and 'description' fields
features.reason: WorkflowMeta is populated from external workflow files; integration test needed to verify parsing produces conformant objects.

features.name: ParallelFn contract
features.description: Verify that the parallel function type contract is upheld: accepts array of thunks and returns Promise of all resolved values in order
features.reason: Parallel execution is a core workflow primitive; incorrect behavior (wrong order, swallowed errors, partial results) breaks workflow semantics.

features.name: PipelineFn contract
features.description: Verify that the pipeline function type contract is upheld: processes items through sequential stages, passing output of one stage as input to the next
features.reason: Pipeline is a core workflow composition primitive; data must flow correctly between stages for workflows to produce correct results.

features.name: AgentFn contract
features.description: Verify that agent function implementations conform to the AgentFn type: accept prompt string and optional AgentOptions, return Promise
features.reason: AgentFn is the primary interface for delegating work to AI agents - the core purpose of the system. Failures here break all workflows.

features.name: WorkflowModule structure
features.description: Verify that WorkflowModule objects contain valid meta (WorkflowMeta) and body (string) when loaded from workflow files
features.reason: WorkflowModule is constructed from external file system resources; integration test verifies the loading/parsing pipeline produces valid structures.

features.name: WorkflowRuntime interface completeness
features.description: Verify that WorkflowRuntime implementations provide all required functions (agent, log, phase, parallel, pipeline) and they are callable
features.reason: WorkflowRuntime is the dependency injection surface for all workflows; missing or non-functional members cause runtime crashes in any workflow.

steps.name: write-report
steps.result: Written to `test-analysis-report.md` — a structured markdown report with all 94 testable features organized into severity-based tables with File, Feature, Test Type, and Reason columns, plus a recommended implementation order at the bottom.

critical.name: executeWorkflow - dynamic function creation
critical.description: Creates an AsyncFunction from the workflow body string with specific parameter names (agent, pipeline, parallel, phase, log, args)
critical.reason: Core execution mechanism - dynamically compiling user-provided code into an async function. Incorrect parameter binding would break all workflows.

critical.name: executeWorkflow - runtime binding
critical.description: Passes runtime properties (agent, pipeline, parallel, phase, log) and args as arguments to the dynamically created function
critical.reason: Core business logic ensuring the runtime API is correctly exposed to workflow scripts. Wrong bindings would silently break workflow execution.

critical.reason: Core business logic orchestrating multiple subsystems: module loading, runtime creation, workflow execution, persistent state management, and sub-agent delegation. A failure here breaks the entire plugin.

critical.name: createEmitResultTool - result capture and unwrapping
critical.description: execute() captures params directly for object schemas, unwraps .result for non-object schemas, and returns terminate:true
critical.reason: Data integrity concern - incorrect unwrapping could corrupt structured output returned to workflows.

critical.name: agent - step lifecycle with schema (structured output)
critical.description: Creates a step with 'running' status, calls onStep, creates agent session with emit_result tool, processes prompt, captures result, marks step as 'completed'
critical.reason: Core workflow execution path involving external agent sessions, tool creation, and state management. Orchestrates multiple components.

critical.name: agent - step lifecycle without schema (text response)
critical.description: Creates agent session without custom tools, subscribes to message events, captures response text, returns it as result
critical.reason: Core workflow execution path involving external agent sessions and event subscription. Primary way workflows interact with LLMs.

critical.name: saveRun - persist workflow run to filesystem
critical.description: Creates the runs directory recursively and writes a WorkflowRun as formatted JSON to a file named by runId

critical.name: saveRun and loadRun roundtrip

critical.name: AgentFn contract
critical.description: Verify that agent function implementations conform to the AgentFn type: accept prompt string and optional AgentOptions, return Promise
critical.reason: AgentFn is the primary interface for delegating work to AI agents - the core purpose of the system. Failures here break all workflows.

high.name: discoverProjectDirs
high.description: Builds list of potential project workflow directories by walking up from cwd to git root, checking standard directory names
high.reason: Depends on findGitRoot and constructs paths relative to filesystem structure; determines which workflows are discoverable

high.name: listDir - error handling
high.description: Returns an empty array when the directory does not exist or cannot be read
high.reason: Graceful error handling is critical for robustness when directories don't exist; silent failure prevents crashes

high.name: listWorkflows - deduplication and priority
high.description: Lists all workflows across directories, deduplicating by name with project workflows taking priority over global ones
high.reason: Core discovery feature; project-over-global priority and dedup logic is critical for correct user experience

high.name: extractMeta - nested braces handling
high.description: Correctly handles nested objects within the meta declaration by tracking brace depth
high.reason: Brace-depth algorithm is error-prone; incorrect parsing would silently break workflow discovery for complex meta objects

high.name: extractMeta - missing or invalid meta
high.description: Returns null when source has no meta export, meta doesn't start with '{', meta is unparseable, or meta lacks a name field
high.reason: Error cases must return null gracefully rather than throwing; guards against malformed workflow files

high.name: loadWorkflow - not found
high.description: Returns null when no workflow with the given name exists in any searched directory with any valid extension
high.reason: Must gracefully handle missing workflows without throwing; important for error UX

high.name: loadWorkflow - skips files without valid meta
high.description: Continues searching when a file exists but extractMeta returns null (file lacks valid meta export)
high.reason: Ensures partially valid files don't block discovery of valid workflows in later directories

high.name: createEmitResultTool - object schema passthrough
high.description: When schema.type is 'object', the schema is passed directly as tool parameters without wrapping
high.reason: Core logic for structured output extraction. Incorrect schema handling would cause validation failures or data loss.

high.name: createEmitResultTool - non-object schema wrapping
high.description: When schema.type is not 'object' (e.g., array, string), wraps in {type:'object', properties:{result:<schema>}, required:['result']}
high.reason: Ensures Claude's object-only tool parameter constraint is satisfied for all schema types.

high.name: agent - abort signal cancellation before start
high.description: If signal is already aborted when agent() is called, throws 'Workflow cancelled' immediately
high.reason: Important for resource cleanup and preventing unnecessary API calls after cancellation.

high.name: agent - abort signal cancellation during execution
high.description: Registers abort handler that calls session.abort() and session.dispose(), marks step as 'cancelled', throws error
high.reason: Ensures proper cleanup of external agent sessions on cancellation. Memory leak potential if mishandled.

high.name: agent - error handling marks step as failed
high.description: When session.prompt throws (non-abort), step status is set to 'failed' with error message, returns undefined
high.reason: Error resilience - workflows must handle LLM failures gracefully without crashing the entire pipeline.

high.name: pipeline - sequential stage processing
high.description: Processes items through multiple stages sequentially, passing output of each stage as input to the next
high.reason: Core data transformation pipeline. Incorrect chaining would produce wrong results in multi-stage workflows.

high.name: pipeline - individual item failure handling
high.description: When a stage throws for one item, logs the error and returns undefined for that item while continuing others
high.reason: Fault tolerance - one failed item shouldn't crash the entire pipeline. Important for batch processing.

high.name: loadRun - read workflow run from filesystem
high.description: Reads a JSON file by runId, parses it, and returns the WorkflowRun object. Returns null if the file doesn't exist or is invalid.
high.reason: Filesystem read with JSON parsing and error handling. Critical path for retrieving run state. Error case (returning null) must be verified.

high.name: loadRun - error handling for missing/invalid files
high.description: Returns null when the file does not exist or contains invalid JSON, rather than throwing
high.reason: Silent error handling means callers depend on null return for missing runs. If this throws instead, it could crash workflow status checks.

high.name: listRuns - list and aggregate all workflow runs
high.description: Reads the runs directory, filters for .json files, loads each run, filters out nulls, and sorts by updatedAt descending
high.reason: Orchestrates multiple filesystem operations (readdir + multiple readFile). Aggregation logic with filtering and sorting is critical for correct UI display.

high.name: listRuns - empty/missing directory handling
high.description: Returns an empty array when the runs directory does not exist, rather than throwing
high.reason: Graceful degradation for fresh projects with no runs. If this throws, workflow list commands would crash on first use.

high.name: WorkflowStep state transitions
high.description: Verify that WorkflowStep objects transition through valid status sequences (pending→running→completed/failed, not completed→pending)
high.reason: State machine correctness is critical for workflow reliability. Invalid transitions could leave workflows in inconsistent states.

high.name: ParallelFn contract
high.description: Verify that the parallel function type contract is upheld: accepts array of thunks and returns Promise of all resolved values in order
high.reason: Parallel execution is a core workflow primitive; incorrect behavior (wrong order, swallowed errors, partial results) breaks workflow semantics.

high.name: PipelineFn contract
high.description: Verify that the pipeline function type contract is upheld: processes items through sequential stages, passing output of one stage as input to the next
high.reason: Pipeline is a core workflow composition primitive; data must flow correctly between stages for workflows to produce correct results.

high.name: WorkflowRuntime interface completeness
high.description: Verify that WorkflowRuntime implementations provide all required functions (agent, log, phase, parallel, pipeline) and they are callable
high.reason: WorkflowRuntime is the dependency injection surface for all workflows; missing or non-functional members cause runtime crashes in any workflow.

medium.name: HTML template serving (/ and /index.html)
medium.description: Serves the HTML template file with correct content-type header at root and /index.html paths
medium.reason: Involves file system read and HTTP response; standard serving behavior but essential for dashboard usability

medium.name: SSE client cleanup on disconnect
medium.description: Removes client from sseClients set when the request connection closes
medium.reason: Prevents memory leaks from accumulating stale client references; requires simulating connection lifecycle

medium.name: File watcher graceful failure
medium.description: Silently handles the case when runsDir does not exist (watch throws) without crashing the server
medium.reason: Defensive coding for missing directory; server should still start even if runs directory doesn't exist yet

medium.name: stop() cleanup function
medium.description: Closes file watcher, ends all SSE connections, clears client set, and closes the HTTP server
medium.reason: Resource cleanup preventing port leaks, zombie watchers, and dangling connections; important for test isolation and graceful shutdown

medium.name: Default port and custom port
medium.description: Uses port 3847 by default but accepts a custom port parameter; returns the actual bound port in the URL
medium.reason: Port configuration affects deployment; actual port from server.address() may differ from requested (e.g., port 0 for random)

medium.name: executeWorkflow - undefined args handling
medium.description: Handles the case where args parameter is undefined
medium.reason: Edge case where no arguments are passed to a workflow. Should pass undefined without errors.

medium.name: formatRun
medium.description: Pure function that formats a WorkflowRun object into a human-readable multi-line string with run ID, workflow name, status, timestamps, steps with durations, and optional result
medium.reason: Pure function with no side effects - transforms structured data to string. Handles optional fields (phase, duration, result) and formats timestamps and durations.

medium.name: workflow tool - list action
medium.description: Lists available workflows from filesystem discovery and recent runs from the store, formatted with source and directory info, plus the script template
medium.reason: Depends on listWorkflows (filesystem discovery across multiple dirs) and listRuns (store access). Combines multiple external data sources into formatted output.

medium.name: workflow tool - status action
medium.description: Retrieves and formats a specific workflow run by run_id, returning error if run_id missing or run not found
medium.reason: Requires loading run state from persistent store (loadRun). Needs validation of run_id parameter and handling of missing runs.

medium.name: workflow tool - start action (workflow not found)
medium.description: When requested workflow doesn't exist, returns helpful error with available workflows list, project dir path, and creation template
medium.reason: Depends on loadWorkflow and listWorkflows for filesystem discovery, plus mkdir for directory creation. Important for UX but not data-critical.

medium.name: workflow tool - args JSON parsing
medium.description: Parses the optional 'args' string parameter as JSON, returning an error if invalid JSON is provided
medium.reason: Input validation logic - parse user-provided JSON string. Invalid input must produce a clear error rather than crashing the workflow start.

medium.name: dashboard command
medium.description: Registers a 'dashboard' command that starts a web dashboard (singleton pattern) and notifies the user of the URL, preventing duplicate instances
medium.reason: Starts an HTTP server (startDashboard) and manages singleton state. Needs to verify deduplication logic and proper server lifecycle.

medium.name: run creation and initialization
medium.description: Creates a new WorkflowRun with randomUUID, workflow name, 'running' status, parsed args, timestamps, and empty steps array before execution begins
medium.reason: Correct initialization of run state is important for downstream tracking. Verifying the shape and defaults ensures store consistency.

medium.name: getAllWorkflowDirs
medium.description: Combines discovered project directories with global directories into a single ordered list
medium.reason: Orchestrates discoverProjectDirs and appends global dirs; order matters for priority resolution

medium.name: listDir - extension filtering
medium.description: Reads a directory and filters files to only those with supported extensions (.js, .ts, .mjs, .mts)
medium.reason: Reads filesystem via readdir, filters by extension; ensures only valid workflow files are included

medium.name: listWorkflows - source labeling
medium.description: Correctly labels each workflow as 'project' or 'global' based on its directory origin
medium.reason: Source labeling affects UI display and user understanding of where workflows come from

medium.name: extractBody
medium.description: Removes the 'export const meta = {...}' declaration from source, returning only the workflow body code
medium.reason: Pure string transformation using regex; important for correct workflow execution but logic is straightforward

medium.name: createEmitResultTool - getResult before execute
medium.description: getResult() returns undefined before execute() is called
medium.reason: Edge case handling for when the model doesn't call the tool.

medium.name: agent - abort handler cleanup on success
medium.description: Removes abort event listener after prompt completes (in finally block) to prevent memory leaks
medium.reason: Memory management concern - leaked listeners could accumulate over many workflow steps.

medium.name: agent - emit_result tool not called fallback
medium.description: If schema is provided but model doesn't call emit_result, step completes with error message 'emit_result tool was not called' and returns undefined
medium.reason: Edge case - defensive handling when model misbehaves. Should rarely happen with terminate:true.

medium.name: agent - schema prompt construction
medium.description: Constructs prompt with schema instructions appended, different wording for object vs non-object schemas
medium.reason: Prompt engineering - incorrect instructions could cause model to fail structured output generation.

medium.name: pipeline - cancellation between stages
medium.description: Checks signal.aborted between stages and throws 'Workflow cancelled'
medium.reason: Ensures long-running pipelines can be cancelled at stage boundaries.

medium.name: pipeline - cancellation within stage tasks
medium.description: Checks signal.aborted before and after each task within a stage, throws on cancellation
medium.reason: Finer-grained cancellation within parallel stage execution.

medium.name: parallel - concurrent execution
medium.description: Runs all thunks concurrently via Promise.all and returns results array
medium.reason: Core concurrency primitive for workflows. Simple but important for parallelizing agent calls.

medium.name: parallel - pre-execution abort check
medium.description: If signal is aborted before starting, throws 'Workflow cancelled' without executing thunks
medium.reason: Prevents wasted work when workflow is already cancelled.

medium.name: createRuntime - returns complete WorkflowRuntime interface
medium.description: Returns object with agent, log, phase, parallel, and pipeline functions matching the WorkflowRuntime interface
medium.reason: Contract verification - ensures runtime satisfies the type interface expected by workflow scripts.

medium.name: listRuns - sorting by updatedAt descending
medium.description: Results are sorted with most recently updated runs first (b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt)
medium.reason: Sort order affects user-facing display. Incorrect ordering would show stale runs first but wouldn't lose data.

medium.name: listRuns - filters only .json files
medium.description: Only files ending in .json are processed; other files in the directory are ignored
medium.reason: Prevents crashes from non-JSON files (e.g., .DS_Store, temp files) in the runs directory.

medium.name: WorkflowStepStatus union type validation
medium.description: Validate that WorkflowStepStatus only accepts 'pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed', or 'cancelled' values at runtime boundaries (e.g., deserialization from JSON, API responses)
medium.reason: String union types are enforced at compile time but not at runtime boundaries where data enters the system (e.g., from disk, API). Runtime validation guards would need testing.

medium.name: WorkflowRun status union type validation
medium.description: Validate that WorkflowRun.status only accepts 'running', 'completed', 'failed', or 'cancelled' values at runtime boundaries
medium.reason: Status field drives workflow control flow; invalid states at runtime boundaries could cause subtle bugs in workflow execution logic.

medium.name: WorkflowRun temporal consistency
medium.description: Verify that createdAt <= updatedAt, and step startedAt <= completedAt invariants hold when constructing or mutating WorkflowRun objects
medium.reason: Temporal invariants ensure correct ordering/auditing of workflow execution. Violations indicate logic bugs in workflow management.

medium.name: WorkflowMeta structure conformance
medium.description: Verify that WorkflowMeta objects parsed from workflow module files have required 'name' and 'description' fields
medium.reason: WorkflowMeta is populated from external workflow files; integration test needed to verify parsing produces conformant objects.

medium.name: WorkflowModule structure
medium.description: Verify that WorkflowModule objects contain valid meta (WorkflowMeta) and body (string) when loaded from workflow files
medium.reason: WorkflowModule is constructed from external file system resources; integration test verifies the loading/parsing pipeline produces valid structures.

low.name: getTemplatePath
low.description: Computes the path to the HTML template file relative to the module location
low.reason: Pure path computation with no side effects; unlikely to break unless file structure changes

low.name: 404 handling for unknown routes
low.description: Returns 404 status with 'Not found' body for any unrecognized URL path
low.reason: Standard fallback behavior; simple but ensures no unintended route exposure

low.name: AsyncFunction constructor
low.description: The AsyncFunction reference is correctly derived from async function prototype
low.reason: Simple constant derivation that is unlikely to change, but validates the metaprogramming approach works correctly.

low.name: session_start - workflow count status
low.description: On session start, discovers workflows in cwd and sets a UI status indicator showing the count
low.reason: Cosmetic UI status update. Depends on filesystem discovery but failure has no functional impact on the extension.

low.name: session_start - ensure project workflow dir
low.description: On session start, creates the project workflow directory (.pi/workflows/) with recursive mkdir, silently catching errors
low.reason: Filesystem side effect with error suppression. Important for first-run experience but failure is gracefully handled and non-critical.

low.name: workflow command - interactive selection
low.description: Registers a 'workflow' command that either lists workflows interactively (with ui.select) or sends a user message to trigger the workflow tool with parsed arguments
low.reason: UI interaction layer that delegates to the workflow tool via sendUserMessage. Logic is thin - mostly string parsing and UI calls.

low.name: getProjectWorkflowDir
low.description: Returns the project workflow directory path by joining cwd with '.pi/workflows'
low.reason: Pure function with simple path joining, no side effects or complex logic

low.name: listWorkflows - alphabetical sorting
low.description: Returns workflows sorted alphabetically by name
low.reason: Cosmetic ordering for display purposes; not functionally critical

low.name: createRuntime - log delegates to ctx.ui.notify
low.reason: Simple delegation with no transformation logic.

low.name: createRuntime - phase sets currentPhase
low.description: phase(name) sets the current phase that is used by subsequent agent calls

low.name: agent - step name derivation
low.description: Step name uses options.label if provided, otherwise first 60 chars of prompt. Phase uses options.phase or currentPhase.
low.reason: Simple string formatting for display purposes.

low.name: runsDir - path construction
low.description: Constructs the correct runs directory path by joining cwd with .pi-workflows/.runs
low.reason: Pure path join function with no branching logic. Unlikely to break unless the constant changes.

low.name: runPath - path construction
low.description: Constructs the correct file path for a run by joining runsDir with {runId}.json
low.reason: Pure path join function. Simple string concatenation with .json extension.

low.name: saveRun - JSON serialization format
low.description: Serializes the run with 2-space indentation and a trailing newline for human readability
low.reason: Formatting is cosmetic. The file is still valid JSON regardless of indentation, but trailing newline follows POSIX conventions.
